message:
feat(stdio): add vis(3) family — strvis/strnvis/strunvis/strnunvis (bd-i18zl)

NetBSD vis(3) — visual byte encoding/decoding used by mtree, fpr,
cd9660, BSD pkg tools, pkgsrc, and many other ports for safely
round-tripping arbitrary binary data through ASCII-only channels
(file names in tar/cpio listings, log lines).

* core::stdio::vis::encode_byte handles the byte-level
  transformation. Default mode emits non-printable bytes as \\^X
  (control chars, with X = c XOR 0x40), \\^? (DEL), or \\M-X
  (high-bit, recursing on the low 7 bits). VIS_OCTAL flag forces
  all non-printable bytes to \\NNN (3-digit octal).
* core::stdio::vis::decode_one inverts the same transformation,
  handling \\\\, \\NNN, \\^X, \\M-X, \\^?, and the C-style short
  escapes \\n \\t \\r \\b \\v \\a \\f. Unknown \\X passes the
  byte through verbatim (matches NetBSD's permissive behavior).
* stdio_abi exposes strvis, strnvis (bounded), strunvis, and
  strnunvis (bounded). NULL pointers and zero-len buffers return
  -1; encode-time overflow on strnvis NUL-terminates the prefix
  that fits.

21 core unit tests covering: every escape form, both encoding
modes, round-trip property over all 256 byte values × both modes
+ 256-byte arbitrary-payload round trip. 12 abi integration tests
covering the four entrypoints, NULL guards, exact-fit truncation,
overflow, malformed-input rejection, end-to-end ABI round trip
through encode + decode. Clippy-clean, fmt-clean. All builds via
rch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
